|2ndRegional cup apps=4|2ndRegional name=[[AFC Challenge Cup]]|2ndRegional cup first=[[2006 AFC Challenge Cup|2006]]|2ndRegional cup best='''Champions''' ([[2006 AFC Challenge Cup|2006]])|3rdRegional cup apps=1|3rdRegional name=[[Football at the Asian Games|Asian Games]]|3rdRegional cup first=[[Football at the 1998 Asian Games|1998]]|3rdRegional cup best=Second round ([[Football at the 1998 Asian Games|1998]])|4thRegional cup apps=2|4thRegional name=[[CAFA Nations Cup]]|4thRegional cup first=[[2023 CAFA Nations Cup|2023]]|4thRegional cup best=Group stage ([[2023 CAFA Nations Cup|2023]])}}

The '''Tajikistan national football team'''{{efn|{{langx|tg|Тими миллии футболи Тоҷикистон|Timi millii futboli Tojikiston}}}} represents [[Tajikistan]] in international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Tajikistan Football Federation]], the governing body for [[football in Tajikistan]]. Tajikistan's homeground is the [[Markazii Jumhuriyavii Stadium]] in [[Dushanbe]].

== History ==
Tajikistan played its first official game on 11 April 1994 in [[Tashkent]] as part of a regional tournament. It lost that game against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] 0–1.<ref name="Member Association - Tajikistan">{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=tjk/fixturesresults/gender=m/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070709070446/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=tjk/fixturesresults/gender=m/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=9 July 2007|title=Member Association - Tajikistan|website=FIFA.com|publisher=FIFA}}</ref> The first official competition the team entered was the [[1996 AFC Asian Cup qualification|qualifiers]] for the [[1996 AFC Asian Cup]], when it was seeded in the 3-team Group 8 with [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]] and [[Bahrain national football team|Bahrain]]. Bahrain later withdrew, leaving Tajikistan to a play-off against their [[Middle Asia]]n neighbors. Tajikistan won the first game 4–0, and were beaten in the away game 0–5 after [[Overtime (sports)|extra time]]. It did not enter a [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] [[FIFA World Cup qualification|qualifying]] tournament until the [[1998 FIFA World Cup]] edition, recording 4 victories in its preliminary group stage including a 5–0 triumph over [[Turkmenistan national football team|Turkmenistan]], and losing only to [[China national football team|China]], which placed it second behind its eastern neighbors, and out of the tournament.<ref name="Member Association - Tajikistan"/>

Following Tajikistan's [[2018 FIFA World Cup]] [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round#Group B|qualifying]] campaign, coach [[Mubin Ergashev]] and his staff were relieved of their duties.<ref name="Ergashev leaves">{{cite web|script-title=ru:Уволен тренерский штаб сборной Таджикистана|url=http://fc-istiklol.tj/index.php?view=1&news=1598|website=fc-istiklol.tj|publisher=FC Istiklol|access-date=31 March 2016|language=ru|date=30 March 2016|archive-date=8 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160408174237/http://fc-istiklol.tj/index.php?view=1&news=1598|url-status=live | title=FC Istiklol }}</ref>

Tajikistan were drawn into a [[2019 AFC Asian Cup]] qualification group with the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]], [[Yemen national football team|Yemen]] and [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]]. Following defeat to the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] on 27 March 2018, confirming Tajikistan's failure to qualify for the Asian Cup, manager [[Khakim Fuzailov]] resigned.<ref name="Fuzailov Resigns">{{cite web|script-title=ru:ХАКИМ ФУЗАЙЛОВ ИЗВИНИЛСЯ ПЕРЕД БОЛЕЛЬЩИКАМИ ЗА НЕВЫХОД СБОРНОЙ ТАДЖИКИСТАНА НА КУБОК АЗИИ-2019 И ПОДАЛ В ОТСТАВКУ|url=http://fft.tj/hakim-fuzajlov-izvinilsya-pered-bolelshhikami-za-nevyhod-sbornoj-tadzhikistana-na-kubok-azii-2019-i-podal-v-otstavku/|website=fft.tj|publisher=Football Federation Tajikistan|access-date=30 March 2018|language=ru|date=27 March 2018|archive-date=30 March 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180330144041/http://fft.tj/hakim-fuzajlov-izvinilsya-pered-bolelshhikami-za-nevyhod-sbornoj-tadzhikistana-na-kubok-azii-2019-i-podal-v-otstavku/|url-status=live}}</ref>

After failing to have a direct spot for the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup]], Tajikistan hired Croatian manager [[Petar Šegrt]], and Tajikistan took part in the third round of the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification]], joining with [[Myanmar national football team|Myanmar]], Kyrgyzstan, and [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]]. Due to [[COVID-19 pandemic]], the third round was played in a single round-robin group format with a centralised host, which Tajikistan's group saw its neighbour [[Kyrgyzstan national football team|Kyrgyzstan]] hosting it. Tajikistan defeated Myanmar (4–0) and Singapore (1–0), before holding neighbour Kyrgyzstan to a goalless draw, topping the group and, after failing in 5 previous attempts, Tajikistan was able to qualify to its first-ever AFC Asian Cup and a major tournament in 2023. Tajikistan was invited to the [[2022 King's Cup]] in Thailand held in September 2022 where Tajikistan defeated [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] 2–1 and sees them advanced to the final defeating [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] in the penalty shootout winning their second international trophy.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nst.com.my/sports/football/2022/09/834395/harimau-malaya-lose-kings-cup-final-after-penalty-shootout-heartbreak|title=Harimau Malaya lose King's Cup final after penalty shootout heartbreak|date=25 September 2022|access-date=26 September 2022|first=Ooi Kin|last=Fai|location=[[Chiang Mai]], Thailand|website=nst.com.my|publisher=[[New Straits Times]]|archive-date=26 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220926080933/https://www.nst.com.my/web/20220926080933/https://www.nst.com.my/sports/football/2022/09/834395/harimau-malaya-lose-kings-cup-final-after-penalty-shootout-heartbreak|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup]], Tajikistan reached the quarter-finals for the first time in their history after losing 1–0 to the eventual runner-up [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]] due to a 66th minute [[own goal]] from [[Vahdat Hanonov]]. Their coach, [[Petar Šegrt]], left his post at the end of this epic campaign due to the expiry of his contract<ref>{{cite web|access-date=10 March 2024 |author=Boris Ghanem |date=18 February 2023 |title=Douche froide à Dushanbe |url=https://lucarne-opposee.fr/index.php/actualite/exp/10279-douche-froide-a-dushanbe |website=lucarne-opposee.fr}}<!-- auto-translated from French by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> and was replaced by his assistant [[Gela Shekiladze]].

In June 2025, it was announced that Tajikistan would co-host the [[2025 CAFA Nations Cup|second edition]] of the [[CAFA Nations Cup]] alongside Uzbekistan.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.qalampir.uz/en/news/uzbekiston-futbol-buyicha-yirik-musobak-aga-mezbonlik-k-iladi-120850 | title=Uzbekistan to host CAFA Nations Cup 2025 }}</ref>

==Kit==
In September 2017, Tajikistan's kit was supplied by Chinese sporting apparel company [[Li-Ning]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://fft.tj/sbornaya-tadzhikistana-sygraet-protiv-nepala-v-beloj-forme/|title=Team of Tajikistan will play against Nepal in white form|language=ru|publisher=Football Federation of Tajikistan|date=4 September 2017|access-date=8 September 2017|archive-date=11 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170911161831/http://fft.tj/sbornaya-tadzhikistana-sygraet-protiv-nepala-v-beloj-forme/|url-status=live}}</ref> 

{{clear}}

==Coaching staff==

[[File:Segrt.JPG|thumb|200px|[[Petar Šegrt]] became the manager of Tajikistan in 2022]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 September 2025 |title=Тренерский штаб национальной и олимпийской сборных Таджикистана покидает свои посты |url=https://fft.tj/trenerskij-shtab-natsionalnoj-i-olimpijskoj-sbornyh-tadzhikistana-pokidaet-svoi-posty/ |website=[[Tajikistan Football Federation]]}}</ref>

===Coaching history===
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Sharif Nazarov]] (1992–1994)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Vladimir Gulyamhaydarov|Vladimir Ghulamhaydarov]] (1994–1995)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[:ru:Мурадов, Абдулло|Abdulla Muradov]] (1996)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[:ru:Бабаев, Зоир|Zair Babayev]] (1997–1998)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Sharif Nazarov]] (1999)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[:ru:Гафуров, Салохиддин Исмаилович|Salahiddin Ghafurov]] (2000–2002)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Sharif Nazarov]] (2003)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[:ru:Бабаев, Зоир|Zair Babayev]] (2004)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Sharif Nazarov]] (2004–2006)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Makhmadjon Khabibulloev|Mahmadjan Habibullayev]] (2007)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[:ru:Кадыров, Пулод|Pulad Qadirov]] (2008–2011)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Alimzhon Rafikov|Alimjan Rafiqov]] (2011–2012)
*{{Flagicon|Bosnia and Herzegovina}} [[Kemal Alispahić]] (2012)
*{{Flagicon|Serbia}} [[Nikola Kavazović]] (2012–2013)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Mubin Ergashev]] (2013)
*{{Flagicon|Russia}} [[Mukhsin Mukhamadiev]] (2013–2015)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Mubin Ergashev]] (2015–2016)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Khakim Fuzailov|Hakim Fuzaylov]] (2016–2018)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} [[Alisher Tukhtaev|Alisher Tuhtayev]] (2018)
*{{Flagicon|Uzbekistan}} [[Usmon Toshev]] (2018–2021)
*{{Flagicon|Tajikistan}} ''[[Mubin Ergashev]]'' (2021)
*{{Flagicon|Croatia}} [[Petar Šegrt]] (2022–2024)
*{{flagicon|GEO}} [[Gela Shekiladze]] (2024–2025)
*{{flagicon|SRB}} [[Goran Stevanović]] (2025–2026)
*{{flagicon|NMK}} '''[[Igor Angelovski]] ''' (2026–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following 31 players were called for Tajikistan's matches against the [[India national football team|India]], on 5 & 9 June.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ҳайати васеи тими миллии Тоҷикистон барои бозиҳои дӯстонаи моҳи июн маълум шуд
 |url=https://varzishtv.tj/tj/news/ayati-vasei-timi-millii-to-ikiston-baroi-bozi-oi-d-stonai-mo-i-iyun-malum-shud/ |website=varzishtv.tj |publisher=Varzish TV |access-date=26 May 2026|language=Tajik |date=23 May 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals updated as of 9 June 2026, after the match against [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]].''

{{nat fs g start}}

<sup>INJ</sup> <span style="font-size:90%">Withdrew due to injury</span><br>
<sup>RET</sup> <span style="font-size:90%">Retired</span><br>
<sup>WTH</sup> <span style="font-size:90%">Withdrawn</span><br>
<sup>PRE</sup> <span style="font-size:90%">Preliminary squad</span><br>
{{nat fs end}}

|align="left"|[[Nuriddin Davronov]]
|9
|51
|{{#expr:9/51 round 2}}
|2008–2021
|-

|52
|{{#expr:9/52 round 2}}
|2006–2015

==Honours==
<!-- DO NOT ADD UNOFFICIAL OR FRIENDLY COMPETITIONS IN THE SUMMARY TABLE FOR OFFICIAL HONOURS ONLY -->
===Continental===
* '''[[AFC Challenge Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup|2006]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2008 AFC Challenge Cup|2008]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup|2010]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[King's Cup (Thailand)|King's Cup]] (1)''': [[2022 King's Cup|2022]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thansettakij.com/sport/541626|title=ผลบอลวันนี้ ทาจิกิสถาน แม่นโทษเอาชนะมาเลเซีย คว้าแชมป์คิงส์คัพไปครอง|trans-title=Today's football results: Tajikistan beats Malaysia on penalties to won the King's Cup Championship|lang=th|work=Thansettakij|date=26 September 2022|access-date=20 October 2023}}</ref>
* '''[[Merdeka Tournament]] (1)''': [[2023 Merdeka Tournament|2023]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://pressa.tj/sbornaya-tadzhikistana-pobeditel-kubka-merdeka-2023/|title=Сборная Таджикистана – победитель «Кубка Мердека-2023»|trans-title=Tajikistan national team is the winner of the Merdeka Cup 2023|lang=ru|work=Pressa.tj|date=15 October 2023|access-date=20 October 2023}}</ref>

===Summary===
Only official honours are included, according to ''FIFA statutes'' (competitions organized/recognized by [[FIFA]] or an affiliated confederation).
